body:
## Summary
- Use the configured agent handle for visible fix-pr completion mentions when the run belongs to an orchestrator chain.
- Avoid double-`@` formatting for preformatted handles.
- Document the orchestrated fix-pr completion mention behavior.

## Tests
- `npm -C .agent run build && node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/response.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

Closes #47

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #52 for issue #47; no review has run yet, so the next useful automated step is code review.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 20/20 | PR keeps status posting in existing `post-comment` / `formatFixPrComment` path and adds only a small shared `formatMention` helper in `.agent/src/response.ts`. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 20/20 | `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` now documents orchestrator-launched fix-pr completion comments using `AGENT_HANDLE`, defaulting to `@sepo-agent`. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 20/20 | The diff changes only the fix-pr status comment `REQUESTED_BY`; the orchestrator handoff path still preserves the original requester context for downstream automation. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 20/20 | The implementation is a one-line workflow expression plus a small formatter guard for already-prefixed handles. No new subsystem or broad abstraction. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 20/20 | Changes are limited to the fix-pr comment mention behavior, focused tests, and matching docs. No unrelated refactors or formatting churn in the diff. |

## Notes

- The PR directly addresses issue #47: orchestrator-launched fix-pr completion comments tag the configured agent handle instead of the original human requester.
- Focused coverage was added for both workflow wiring and the `@@sepo-agent` double-prefix case.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #52 addresses issue #47, where orchestrator-launched `fix-pr` completion comments were tagging the original human requester instead of the Sepo agent handle. The change updates the workflow status-comment environment so orchestrated fix-pr runs use `AGENT_HANDLE`, defaulting to `@sepo-agent`. It also adds mention formatting so preformatted handles do not become double-prefixed, plus focused tests and orchestrator documentation. The goal is a visible status comment that reflects agent-to-agent orchestration without changing the underlying requester authorization context.

## Review
Both reviewer outputs found no blocking or warning-level issues. The implementation is narrow, covered by tests, and consistent with the requested behavior and applicable rubrics.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues found | INFO | Reviews found no required branch changes. No inline comments were posted because there were no concrete file/line-specific findings. |

## Progress
- Existing PR discussion already records the automation handoff and the successful implementation summary.
- Rubrics review passed with a 100 score and no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Existing inline review comments were checked; none were present.
- No reviewer disagreement was found.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: No unresolved actionable work remains for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #52.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #52, its issue/PR comments, reviews, diff, linked issue #47, contributor trust signals, and the existing rubric files. The PR conversation contains only bot-authored comments, no inline reviews, and no trusted maintainer instruction to add or update a durable rubric. The linked issue is a one-off product behavior request, not a reusable user/team work preference, and existing rubrics already cover the relevant workflow expectations.

title: Mention agent for orchestrated fix-pr completion
